name: Etzel
description: Etzel, also known as Attila the Hun in historical accounts, is a prominent figure in Germanic heroic legend, particularly in the Nibelungenlied and the Volsunga saga. While based on the historical Attila, the legendary Etzel is often portrayed with a mix of fearsome power and noble qualities. He is depicted as a wealthy and powerful king of the Huns, ruling over a vast empire and commanding a formidable army. In the Nibelungenlied, Etzel marries Kriemhild, the widow of Siegfried, as part of her elaborate plan for revenge against her brothers, the Burgundians. Etzel, though initially unaware of Kriemhild's intentions, becomes entangled in the bloody conflict that ensues, resulting in the devastating destruction of both the Burgundian and Hunnic armies. Despite the tragic consequences, Etzel is often presented as a generous and hospitable host, bound by the customs of his time. His character serves as a vehicle for exploring themes of revenge, fate, and the clash of cultures. The discrepancies between the historical Attila and the legendary Etzel are significant, reflecting the process of mythologization and the adaptation of historical figures to serve narrative purposes. Etzel's role in the Nibelungenlied highlights the destructive nature of vengeance and the devastating consequences of unchecked ambition, even for powerful rulers. He ultimately mourns the loss of his people and the destruction wrought by Kriemhild's actions, solidifying his position as a tragic figure caught in the web of fate and revenge.
subtitle: King of the Huns, Husband of Kriemhild

keyMyths.title: Etzel's Marriage to Kriemhild
keyMyths.description: Kriemhild marries Etzel as part of her plan to lure her brothers, the Burgundians, to his court and exact revenge for Siegfried's death. The marriage is a political alliance with devastating consequences.

keyMyths.title: The Destruction of the Burgundians
keyMyths.description: Etzel's court becomes the site of a bloody battle when Kriemhild instigates a conflict between the Huns and the visiting Burgundians, leading to their annihilation. Etzel is devastated by the loss of his people.

keyMyths.title: Etzel's Lament
keyMyths.description: After the massacre, Etzel mourns the loss of his warriors and expresses sorrow for the destruction that has befallen his kingdom due to Kriemhild's thirst for revenge. He is left a broken and grieving king.

sources.author: Anonymous
